jokes.text: Or facing a bitch of a commute.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Rachel will be moving to Paris for the job
jokes.punchline: Chandler deadpans about the impossibly long commute from NYC to Paris
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler timing and delivery, classic understatement of a massive life change. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: My boss said I might be getting a new lamp in my cubicle.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions his boss will do anything to accommodate their Paris situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically contrasts with his own pathetic workplace 'perk'
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace humor contrast, very Chandler-esque sarcasm about corporate life

jokes.text: No, no, no! No, no, no! No, no, too much is changing, okay? First Phoebe getting married. Congratulations. And then these two move in to a stupid house in the stupid suburbs.
jokes.setup: Rachel announces her Paris job
jokes.punchline: Joey's childlike panic about change, interrupting his own complaint to congratulate Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Great character-driven panic, the polite interruption for congratulations is perfectly Joey

jokes.text: Earlier, you said you'd be supportive. - It comes and goes. I wouldn't trust it.
jokes.setup: Monica calls out Chandler's inconsistent support
jokes.punchline: Chandler honestly admits his unreliability with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware comedy, perfect Chandler delivery and timing. The honesty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No,no.No. No, my hugs are reserved for people staying in America.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to hug Joey after asking for support
jokes.punchline: Joey creates an absurd patriotic hug policy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Joey logic, turning emotional moment into patriotic comedy

jokes.text: Hey! No! Get your France-going arms away from me!
jokes.setup: Continuation of Joey's anti-France stance
jokes.punchline: Escalates to treating Rachel's arms as contaminated by her France plans
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the hug bit, though less strong than the initial setup

jokes.text: Talking to Ross. - I see.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Ross is okay with Rachel leaving, clarifies she meant Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's simple, slightly hurt acknowledgment of being overlooked
jokes.explanation: Small character moment, Ross feeling overlooked. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: How's that gonna happen? Is this the best way to use one of your three magic wishes?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests talking to Rachel's boss might get her old job back
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically frames it as wasting a fairy tale wish
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm with creative fairy tale reference to mock the unrealistic plan

jokes.text: The guy who kept calling you 'Ron'? - I didn't say we were brothers!
jokes.setup: Monica questions how well Ross knows Rachel's boss
jokes.punchline: Ross defensively minimizes the name confusion as if it's still a connection
jokes.explanation: Ross's desperate justification of a weak connection is endearingly pathetic

jokes.text: We're not very bright.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why they shouldn't look at another house
jokes.punchline: Bluntly admits their intellectual limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and self-deprecating honesty, very Chandler

jokes.text: Well, I think that shirt makes you look like you should work at a Baskin-Robbins.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Phoebe's opinion about house hunting
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely ignores the question to insult Monica's shirt
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe randomness and directness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: She was the first black man to fly solo across the Atlantic. - Oh, wait a minute, I read the wrong one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe reads about Estelle's death
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reads Charles Lindbergh's obituary, creating absurd description
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and absurdist humor, creates memorable moment with perfect Phoebe confusion

jokes.text: I know. He always wanted to be the first black man to cross the Atlantic.
jokes.setup: Following Phoebe's mix-up about Estelle's accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Monica plays along with the absurd description applied to Joey
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but less funny than the original mistake, extends the bit

jokes.text: Unless Snoopy says it to Charlie Brown, I think we're okay.
jokes.setup: Concern that Joey might read about Estelle's death in the newspaper
jokes.punchline: Monica jokes about Joey's limited reading habits via Peanuts reference
jokes.explanation: Clever character observation about Joey's reading level, creative pop culture reference

jokes.text: Oh, right. Don? - Close. Ron.
jokes.setup: Ross reminds Rachel's boss they met at Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Boss still gets Ross's name wrong, Ross corrects to the wrong name he's used to
jokes.explanation: Good callback to establish the running gag, Ross's resignation to wrong name is funny

jokes.text: She is good. - Huh. I took a shot there.
jokes.setup: Ross claims Rachel was irreplaceable at her job
jokes.punchline: Ross admits he was just guessing about her competence
jokes.explanation: Ross's honest admission undercuts his persuasive attempt, endearingly awkward

jokes.text: Oh, that's not true. There's nothing I want to do.
jokes.setup: Boss says there's nothing he can do to rehire Rachel
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to admit he just doesn't want to help
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expected polite refusal with blunt honesty

jokes.text: Is this your son? - Yeah, his name is Ross.
jokes.setup: Ross notices photo on boss's desk
jokes.punchline: Boss's son coincidentally has the same name as Ross
jokes.explanation: Relies on coincidence for humor, sets up the dinosaur connection but not particularly clever

jokes.text: What? - Nothing, it's just that it's close to Ron.
jokes.setup: Ross reacts to hearing his own name
jokes.punchline: Boss explains the connection to the wrong name he's been calling Ross
jokes.explanation: Extends the name gag but doesn't add much new comedy

jokes.text: I just heard it how you must have heard it, and that's not good.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes his museum offer sounded inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Ross acknowledges how creepy his invitation sounded
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy, Ross catching his own awkwardness is relatable and funny

jokes.text: Let me start again. I'm a paleontologist. You'll be there with us, and the touching refers only to bones-- Fossils!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to clarify his museum invitation
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by emphasizing 'touching' and 'bones' before correcting to fossils
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross digging himself deeper, perfectly awkward escalation of the misunderstanding

jokes.text: He's always reading, collecting rocks and he's obsessed with dinosaurs. - He'll be fine!
jokes.setup: Boss worries about his nerdy son
jokes.punchline: Ross enthusiastically dismisses concerns about traits that perfectly describe himself
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Ross hearing his own interests described as problems and reassuring the parent

jokes.text: Because a promise between friends means never having to give a reason.
jokes.setup: Joey asks why he should wait to call Estelle
jokes.punchline: Phoebe delivers fake wisdom that sounds profound but makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe pseudo-wisdom, sounds meaningful but is actually nonsensical

jokes.text: I love that saying!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's nonsensical 'wisdom'
jokes.punchline: Joey is genuinely impressed by the meaningless statement
jokes.explanation: Good Joey reaction, his gullibility makes Phoebe's fake wisdom funnier

jokes.text: It's a little coincidental, but believable.
jokes.setup: Joey says he was just going to call Estelle when 'she' calls
jokes.punchline: Joey analyzes the believability of the plot coincidence
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on sitcom plotting, though Joey wouldn't typically be this aware

jokes.text: I guess I dropped the ball there. Whoopsie!
jokes.setup: Joey complains about not getting auditions
jokes.punchline: Phoebe-as-Estelle casually dismisses career negligence with childish 'whoopsie'
jokes.explanation: Good character work, captures Estelle's cavalier attitude toward Joey's career

jokes.text: Don't take that tone with me. Who do you think you are? Alan Lemon, the first black man to fly solo across the Atlantic?
jokes.setup: Joey gets angry at fake Estelle
jokes.punchline: Phoebe incorporates her earlier newspaper mix-up into the Estelle impression
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes both jokes funnier, creative integration of earlier mistake

jokes.text: Man, tough week for Estelle.
jokes.setup: Joey just fired Estelle (who's actually dead)
jokes.punchline: Phoebe deadpans about Estelle's 'rough week' of dying and being fired
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy understatement, great economy and timing

jokes.text: Stop forcing that thing on her.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions Emma can't sleep without her T-rex
jokes.punchline: Ross suspects Rachel of pushing dinosaurs on their daughter
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Ross's dinosaur obsession projecting onto parenting

jokes.text: I mean, that place must just be falling apart without me!
jokes.setup: Rachel can't understand why Ralph Lauren called her back
jokes.punchline: Rachel assumes they're desperate without her (not knowing Ross orchestrated it)
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows why she was rehired while Rachel misunderstands

jokes.text: [ROSS BABBLING INCOHERENTLY]
jokes.setup: Rachel says she's still going to Paris despite the job offer
jokes.punchline: Ross's plan backfires, leaving him speechless and babbling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy reaction showing Ross's shock, relies more on David Schwimmer's performance

jokes.text: If we're gonna cheat, shouldn't it be with a hot, younger house that does stuff that our house won't do?
jokes.setup: Monica says looking at another house feels like cheating
jokes.punchline: Chandler extends the metaphor to marital infidelity
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor with good structure, typical Chandler sexual humor

jokes.text: What a hole! - So, what do you think? - Love it.
jokes.setup: Monica criticizes the house as terrible
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells agent she loves it when asked directly
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Monica's politeness overriding honesty

jokes.text: [MONICA GASPS AND GRABS CHANDLER]
jokes.setup: Monica sees someone upstairs
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic physical reaction suggests she's seen something terrible
jokes.explanation: Generic physical reaction, sets up the Janice reveal but not independently funny

jokes.text: A coronary embolism might.
jokes.setup: Joey says firing won't kill Estelle
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly mentions what actually did kill her
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy, Phoebe's casual medical knowledge adds to the morbid humor

jokes.text: Except for this guy who eats paper. And I'm guessing he eats more money than he makes.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions Estelle's other client
jokes.punchline: Joey makes a pun about the paper-eater's profitability
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with economic logic, surprisingly clever for Joey

jokes.text: Because patience is the road to understanding. Which is the key to a happy heart.
jokes.setup: Joey asks why he should wait for Estelle to call
jokes.punchline: Phoebe delivers more fake profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Continues the fake wisdom bit but less effectively than before

jokes.text: You blow me away.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's continued fake wisdom
jokes.punchline: Joey remains completely convinced by meaningless statements
jokes.explanation: Extends the gullible Joey bit but adds little new

jokes.text: And that means never having to give a reason.
jokes.setup: Joey promises to wait
jokes.punchline: Phoebe repeats her earlier nonsensical 'wisdom'
jokes.explanation: Callback feels forced, doesn't add much to the original joke

jokes.text: What a small world! And yet I never run into Beyoncé.
jokes.setup: Running into Janice at the house viewing
jokes.punchline: Chandler contrasts unwanted encounters with desired celebrity meetings
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about life's ironic coincidences, contemporary reference works

jokes.text: Before Monica made an honest man out of him, Chandler used to be my little love muffin. [LAUGHING NASALLY]
jokes.setup: Janice explains her relationship with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Uses embarrassing pet name with her signature annoying laugh
jokes.explanation: Classic Janice awkwardness, the pet name and laugh create perfect cringe

jokes.text: Why? - I don't know why.
jokes.setup: Janice asks why they're buying next door
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked, honest response reveals his mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good panic response from Chandler, shows how Janice rattles him

jokes.text: The only way that that is going to happen is if the other couple are the Hitlers!
jokes.setup: Monica suggests maybe another couple will get the house instead of Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler claims only literal Nazis could be worse neighbors than Janice
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Chandler's desperation, absurd but perfectly character-driven

jokes.text: By the way, I don't mean to sound distasteful, but when did you start crapping money?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests buying both houses
jokes.punchline: Chandler bluntly questions their sudden wealth with crude imagery
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm with memorable crude metaphor

jokes.text: Just the crickets and, 'Oh, my God!'
jokes.setup: Discussion of how loud Janice will be in the suburbs
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagines peaceful country sounds interrupted by Janice's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between peaceful nature and Janice's intrusion, great imagery and economy

jokes.text: Never again will you have to hear the three words that make your balls jump back up inside your body!
jokes.setup: Monica argues for eliminating surprise Janice encounters
jokes.punchline: Monica describes Chandler's physical fear response to Janice
jokes.explanation: Vivid, crude imagery perfectly captures Chandler's terror, memorable phrase

jokes.text: Oh, that does it too.
jokes.setup: Janice's laugh is heard off-screen
jokes.punchline: Chandler confirms the laugh also triggers his fear response
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy, extends the physical comedy

jokes.text: What if you could give your son this genuine pterodactyl egg replica.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to sweeten the rehiring deal
jokes.punchline: Ross presents a clearly fake 'replica' as if it's valuable
jokes.explanation: Good Ross character moment, his paleontology obsession making him overvalue cheap replicas

jokes.text: Believe me, the ladies, they love it. - I meant with my son. - Good, because the ladies, not so much.
jokes.setup: Boss says the egg will make him popular
jokes.punchline: Ross assumes he means romantically, then admits his romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and self-deprecating Ross humor about his dating struggles

jokes.text: I didn't think I should just drop by. - No, no! Don't drop by! Don't drop by! No.No.No.
jokes.setup: Fake Estelle says she won't drop by
jokes.punchline: Joey panics at the thought of dead Estelle visiting
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy - Joey's innocent terror at supernatural implications

jokes.text: 'Out of area.' Boy, I'll say.
jokes.setup: Joey checks caller ID after 'Estelle's' call
jokes.punchline: Joey unknowingly makes a profound understatement about death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect economy. Joey's innocent observation about the afterlife is genius-level writing.

jokes.text: The Hitlers will be so disappointed.
jokes.setup: Janice announces they're making an offer on the house
jokes.punchline: Chandler continues treating the Hitlers as real competing buyers
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that extends the absurd comparison, though less impactful than original

jokes.text: I never stopped loving you.
jokes.setup: Chandler acts happy about Janice being their neighbor
jokes.punchline: Chandler pretends to still be romantically interested in Janice
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Chandler's desperate scheme, creates maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: I want you. I need you. I must have you, Janice Litman-Goralnik neé Hosenstein.
jokes.setup: Chandler's fake romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top romantic language combined with Janice's ridiculous full name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake seduction, the full name adds comedy through absurd formality

jokes.text: Don't dangle the dream and take it away.
jokes.setup: Janice says they shouldn't buy the house after Chandler's declaration
jokes.punchline: Chandler desperately tries to maintain the charade while actually getting what he wants
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as Chandler has to argue against what he actually wants

jokes.text: [CHANDLER AND JANICE KISS]
jokes.setup: Chandler's fake seduction scheme
jokes.punchline: Chandler has to actually kiss Janice to sell the performance
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff of the scheme, relies on visual disgust rather than wit

jokes.text: They're never coming down now!
jokes.setup: Chandler's traumatic experience kissing Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler refers back to his fear response from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off the earlier physical comedy setup

jokes.text: And that I should thank some 'Ron.' I don't even know what department that guy's in!
jokes.setup: Rachel explains Ralph Lauren's new offer
jokes.punchline: Rachel unknowingly credits Ross while still getting his name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony and callback, Rachel crediting 'Ron' without knowing it's Ross

jokes.text: You should go. - What? - It's what you want. You should go.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains her conflicted feelings about Paris vs staying
jokes.punchline: Ross does the opposite of what he's been scheming for - tells her to follow her dreams
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment for Ross, subverts his earlier scheming with genuine support

jokes.text: Dude, where's my speech?
jokes.setup: Joey and Al are at Estelle's memorial as her only clients
jokes.punchline: Joey realizes he's unprepared while the paper-eating guy performs
jokes.explanation: Decent Joey moment showing his lack of preparation, closes out the Estelle storyline

jokes.text: That is entertaining. Al Zebooker, everybody!
jokes.setup: Al presumably performs his paper-eating act at the memorial
jokes.punchline: Joey treats the solemn memorial like a variety show
jokes.explanation: Good inappropriate response from Joey, treating memorial like entertainment
